slides.title: What is a Subset?
slides.text:
Set A is a Subset of Set B if ALL members of A are also inside B.

Think of it like a smaller box sitting inside a bigger box.

slides.title: The Notation (⊂)
slides.text:
We use the symbol ⊂ to mean 'is a subset of'.

A ⊂ B

This is read as: Set A is a subset of Set B.

slides.title: The Empty Set (Ø)
slides.text:
Before we count subsets, we must meet the Empty Set.

This is a set with NO members. It is empty! We write it as {} or Ø.

slides.title: The Golden Rule
slides.text:
Important Rule: The Empty Set is a subset of EVERY set.

Why? Because 'nothing' can fit inside anything!

Ø ⊂ A

slides.title: Calculating Subsets
slides.text:
To find the total number of subsets, use the formula:

Subsets = 2ⁿ

Where n is the number of elements in the set.

slides.title: Let's Verify!
slides.text:
If Set V = { a, b }, then n = 2.
2² = 4 Subsets.

Let's list them:
1. {a, b} (The set itself)
2. {a}
3. {b}
4. { } (The Empty Set!)
